description_long: This bungalow features a wide, covered front porch supported by columns and a set of steps leading up to the main entrance. The exterior is clad in horizontal siding painted a muted green, with contrasting trim and a dark, gabled roof. The roof extends over the porch, providing shelter and architectural interest, and includes a secondary, smaller gable on the side. Windows are large and rectangular, symmetrically placed on the sides and rear, with a pair of windows flanking the front door. The structure sits on a raised foundation, with a concrete or stone base visible at the bottom. The overall design is compact and single-story, typical of early 20th-century American residential architecture.
description: A single-story green bungalow with a wide front porch, gabled roof, and symmetrical window arrangement. The house features horizontal siding, prominent trim, and a raised foundation.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wide covered porch, central steps, and main entrance flanked by columns.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the gabled roof, two large side windows, and the triangular attic window.
description_view_2: Rear view with a plain facade, two large windows, and a back door, lacking porch details.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with similar gable and window arrangement as the other side, showing the porch's side entry.
attribute_window_arrangement: symmetrical front-facing windows with paired side windows
